title: Summary Ranges
description:
Summary Ranges

You are given a sorted unique integer array nums.

A range [a,b] is the set of all integers from a to b (inclusive).

Return the smallest sorted list of ranges that cover all the numbers in the array exactly. That is, each element of nums is covered by exactly one of the ranges, and there is no integer x such that x is in one of the ranges but not in nums.

Each range [a,b] in the list should be output as:

  • "a->b" if a != b
  • "a" if a == b

Examples:

  • Input: nums = [0,1,2,4,5,7]
    Output: ["0->2","4->5","7"]
    Explanation: The ranges are [0,2], [4,5], and [7].
  • Input: nums = [0,2,3,4,8,10,11]
    Output: ["0","2->4","8","10->11"]
    Explanation: The ranges are [0,0], [2,4], [8,8], and [10,11].

Constraints:

  • 0 <= nums.length <= 20
  • -2^31 <= nums[i] <= 2^31 - 1
  • All values of nums are unique
  • nums is sorted in ascending order
solution_text:
Summary Ranges

Algorithm:

  1. Use two pointers to track the start and end of each range
  2. Walk through the sorted array
  3. When the next number is not consecutive, close the current range
  4. Format each range as "a->b" or "a"

Time Complexity: O(n)

Space Complexity: O(1) excluding output

Key Insights:

  • Since the array is sorted and unique, consecutive numbers differ by exactly 1
  • Track the start of each range and extend until a gap is found
